title: salvia pratensis var. caerulea
intro: Salvia pratensis var. caerulea, also known as blue meadow sage, is a perennial herb that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving cognitive function, and supporting respiratory health. Blue meadow sage can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be consumed orally or used topically to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
